title: Inline modules
instruction:
Define a module with `mod`. Items inside are private by default.

Run this.
successMessage: ::  navigates module paths. hello is pub — accessible outside. secret is private — only code inside the module can call it.

title: Nested modules
instruction:
Modules can be nested. Paths use :: separators.

Run this.
successMessage: Nested modules create a hierarchy. Each level needs pub to be accessible from outside.

title: use to shorten paths
instruction:
`use` brings a path into scope so you don't have to repeat it.

Run this.
successMessage: use imports the name into the current scope. Now you can write circle_area instead of geometry::circle_area.

title: use with as
instruction:
`as` renames an import to avoid conflicts or shorten long names.

Run this.
successMessage: as gives an alias. HashMap as Map and HashSet as Set are local renames. Useful when two crates export the same name.

title: super and self
instruction:
`super` refers to the parent module. `self` refers to the current module.

Run this.
successMessage: super::outer_fn() reaches up to the parent module. This is how inner modules call sibling or parent functions.

title: pub struct fields
instruction:
Struct fields are private by default. Mark them `pub` individually.

Run this.
successMessage: host and port are pub — readable outside. secret_key is private — only Config's methods can access it. Struct encapsulation in Rust.

title: use glob imports
instruction:
`use module::*` imports everything public from a module.

Use sparingly — it can cause name conflicts.

Run this.
successMessage: Glob imports bring everything public into scope. Convenient in tests (use super::*) but risky in production code.

title: External crates
instruction:
External crates are listed in Cargo.toml. Use them with `use`.

The standard library (std) is always available.

Run this.
successMessage: Brace imports use a::b::{X, Y} to import multiple items from the same path. BTreeMap keeps keys sorted — HashMap does not.
